cycleContext.note: June 6 scan (14-day window: 2026-05-23–2026-06-06). Key signals: (1) Bolivia military crackdown law signed June 8 — 10 killed, cities blockaded; (2) DRC Ebola 515/91, Uganda 19/2 with Kampala urban spread; (3) Iran 124th prison hunger-strike week, new capital sentences, 612+ executions YTD; (4) Oracle WARN Act June 15 deadline — workers face sign-or-forfeit ultimatum; (5) ICE June 4 rescinded death-reporting policy for released detainees; (6) India: ~5,000 Bengali Muslims expelled without due process; (7) UAE documented arming Sudan RSF genocide; (8) Haiti record 1.5M displaced — hurricane season begun; (9) Yemen: HRW/Amnesty called for release of 73 Houthi-detained UN staff (2nd anniversary); (10) Health insurer price-fixing cartel: Arizona AG + Lifepoint + DOJ grand jury all active.

newMaterialEventsInWindow: June 7-8: Bolivia military crackdown law passed and signed — 10 killed, 365 arrested, La Paz/El Alto blockaded
June 5: IOM — Haiti displacement hits record 1.5 million (majority women and girls)
June 8: UAE-Sudan arms documentation published — UAE supplying weapons/mercenaries to RSF (genocide in Darfur)
June 5-6: Ebola Bundibugyo — DRC 515/91; Uganda 19/2 with Kampala urban spread
June 8: India confirmed 5,000 Bengali Muslim deportations — West Bengal CM acknowledged 4,800 deported
June 2: Lifepoint hospital system filed federal "MultiPlan Cartel" complaint naming Aetna, Cigna, Elevance, Kaiser
June 9: Iran "No to Executions Tuesdays" enters 124th consecutive week; new capital sentences for 3 political prisoners
June 2: Venezuela HRW universal jurisdiction report — recommends third-country prosecutions for crimes against humanity

prioritizedEntities.name: Iran
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: 124th consecutive week of "No to Executions Tuesdays" prison campaign across 56 facilities. Fresh capital sentences issued for Yasin Shahbakhsh, Hassan Mosalavi, and Ali Kamali. Four political detainees in Sheiban/Ahvaz prison face imminent execution. At least 49 hanged since late May. NCRI: 612+ executions in 2026 YTD. Surge follows 12-day Iran-US-Israel war; rights groups document mass arbitrary arrests.

prioritizedEntities.name: Oracle Corporation
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: June 15 WARN Act severance-release deadline for ~30,000 laid-off workers. Oracle absorbed 60-day WARN pay into severance, leaving newer workers with zero net payout. Workers face sign-or-forfeit ultimatum. Retroactive "remote" reclassification to avoid WARN obligations disputed. Active state investigations in WA and MO. Federal WARN Act entitles qualifying workers to 60 days back pay.

prioritizedEntities.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Ebola Bundibugyo: as of June 6, DRC 515 confirmed cases / 91 deaths. Spread to Uganda: 19 confirmed / 2 deaths, 8 of 9 Uganda cases in Kampala. WHO PHEIC active. CDC worst-case 20,000 cases. Only 20% of contacts traced. No approved vaccine. Concurrent 26.5M acute hunger. Ituri province hardest hit (487 cases across 17 health zones).

prioritizedEntities.name: Bolivia
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: June 7: Chamber of Deputies passed law allowing military force against protesters with presumption-of-legality shield. June 8: President Paz signed law. 10 killed, 365+ arrested as of June 9. La Paz and El Alto nearly fully blockaded. June 2: Defense + Education ministers resigned. Terrorism charges vs 25 union leaders. COB executive secretary Mario Argullo charged. Cities isolated. Worst economic crisis in 4 decades.

prioritizedEntities.name: Russia
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: June 2: Massive aerial attack on Kyiv — 22 civilians killed including 3-year-old child and 8-year-old. 138 wounded. 656 drones + 73 missiles. Dnipro apartment building collapse. Putin rejected direct peace talks June 6. UN verified 60,659 civilian casualties (15,850 killed) since Feb 2022. Ukraine air defense shortage from depleted US stocks worsening civilian exposure.

prioritizedEntities.name: United States
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: June 4: ICE Acting Director memo ended requirement to publicly report deaths of detainees within 30 days of release. 18 detainee deaths in first 5 months of 2026 (up from 33-49 in all of 2025, highest rate in 2+ decades). Detained population: 60,000-70,000. Oversight bodies lose accountability record. Bolivia: 5,000 Bengali Muslims deported from West Bengal without due process. June 5 confirmed policy change.

prioritizedEntities.name: UnitedHealth Group
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Active DOJ criminal and civil Medicare Advantage fraud probe (inflated risk scores, diagnoses added without physician confirmation). Company launched third-party review of business policies. Arizona AG June 1 named UnitedHealthcare in 8-insurer price-fixing cartel suit (MultiPlan algorithm). Lifepoint hospital system filed separate June 2 federal complaint naming same cartel. DOJ Antitrust Division investigating MultiPlan — grand jury subpoena confirmed May 18 SEC filing.

prioritizedEntities.name: India
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Nearly 5,000 Bengali-speaking Muslim men, women, and children deported to Bangladesh since late May. West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari confirmed 4,800 deported + 836 in detention awaiting deportation. Rights groups: many confirmed Indian citizens expelled without citizenship verification. Police detained Bengali-speaking Muslims, labelled undocumented, handed to border forces — often despite producing Indian identity documents. UN condemned as discriminatory.

prioritizedEntities.name: Israel
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Post-ceasefire (Oct 2025): 856 Palestinians killed since ceasefire. Weekly trucks fell from 4,200 to 590 after Feb 28 crossings closure. 37+ aid organizations banned. HRW May 2026: "Israel curbs aid, kills civilians during ceasefire." No Gaza hospital fully operational. ICC arrest warrants for Netanyahu and Gallant maintained despite Israeli legal challenges. West Bank: 36,000+ forcibly displaced, 1,732 settler violence incidents.

prioritizedEntities.name: Sudan
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Civil war year 4. 28M+ facing acute food insecurity. RSF El Fasher: UN confirmed 6,000+ killings with "hallmarks of genocide" against Zaghawa and Fur communities. 11.8M displaced. UAE documented supplying weapons and mercenaries to RSF. International aid only 40% funded. June 8: Daily Kos investigation — UAE bankrolling genocide. Lean season June-August worsening.

prioritizedEntities.name: Myanmar
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: June 1: Junta jets dropped 4 heavy bombs on Kyaung Cha Twin IDP camp, Ramree Township, Rakhine State at 9:30pm while residents slept. 2 civilians killed (men in their 50s), 5+ wounded. Separate IDP camp strikes in Taungup township same night. Pattern: ~100 civilians killed in junta airstrikes since March 16 sham parliament opening. Multiple fighter jets deployed against single targets in barrages.

prioritizedEntities.name: Yemen
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: June 7: HRW + Amnesty called for immediate release of 73 UN and civil society staff detained by Houthis since 2024. UN Security Council June 6 press statement condemned detentions on 2nd anniversary. WFP staffer died in custody Feb 2025. WFP terminated 365 Yemen staff contracts due to Houthi restrictions. 22.3M Yemenis need humanitarian assistance. 18.3M acutely food insecure.

prioritizedEntities.name: Haiti
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: June 5: IOM reported 1.5 million displaced — record high, majority women and girls. May: 18,000+ displaced in Cité Soleil alone. Port-au-Prince above 300,000 IDP for first time on record. Atlantic hurricane season began June 1 with 110,000 Haitians forcibly returned from abroad since start of 2026. Southeast Department newly a displacement hotspot. Severe shortages of shelter, food, water, healthcare.

prioritizedEntities.name: Amazon.com
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: May 29: NLRB Administrative Law Judge ruled Amazon violated NLRA on 4 counts at DCK6 last-mile station (San Francisco Bayview). Ordered reinstatement + back pay + expungement for terminated worker. NLRB previously ordered Amazon to bargain with Teamsters Local 1 (Staten Island). March 31 settlement: Amazon agreed to stop draining UPT balances during strikes. NLRB quorum dysfunction (Jan 2025-Jan 2026) delayed enforcement.

prioritizedEntities.name: 3M Company
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: May 28: Australia filed A$2B ($1.43B) PFAS lawsuit against 3M — largest ever brought by Australian government. Centers on firefighting foam contamination at military sites; Defence Dept spent A$1.3B cleaning up contamination. 3M will defend. 15,232 US personal injury claims pending in federal MDL. July 31 deadline for water system Phase 2 claims. Minnesota MPCA new suit over Cottage Grove contamination.

sector_alerts.title: Bolivia Military Powers Law Signed — Protest Crackdown Escalating
sector_alerts.summary: June 7-8: Bolivian legislature passed and President Paz signed law granting military power against protesters with presumption-of-legality shield. 10 killed, 365+ arrested. La Paz and El Alto blockaded. 7th week of protests. Terrorism charges against 25 union leaders. Worst political-economic crisis in 4 decades.

sector_alerts.title: Ebola Bundibugyo PHEIC — DRC 515 Cases / Uganda Kampala Spread
sector_alerts.summary: As of June 6: DRC 515 confirmed/91 deaths; Uganda 19 confirmed/2 deaths with Kampala urban spread confirmed. CDC worst-case 20,000 cases. WHO PHEIC active. Only 20% contacts traced. No approved vaccine. Cross-entity risk for DRC, Uganda, and neighboring countries.

sector_alerts.title: ICE Detainee Death Reporting Rescinded — June 4 Policy Change
sector_alerts.summary: ICE Acting Director memo (June 4) ended requirement to publicly report deaths within 30 days of release. 18 detainee deaths Jan-May 2026 (highest rate in 20+ years). Detained population 60,000-70,000. Structural transparency rollback eliminates oversight of post-release deaths linked to detention conditions.

sector_alerts.title: India West Bengal — 5,000 Bengali Muslims Expelled Without Due Process
sector_alerts.summary: Following BJP West Bengal election win, ~5,000 Bengali-speaking Muslims expelled to Bangladesh. Confirmed Indian citizens pushed across border without court hearing despite producing identity documents. HRW condemned as discriminatory. UN: violates international law. 836 more in detention centers awaiting deportation.

sector_alerts.title: Health Insurer Price-Fixing Cartel — MultiPlan DOJ Grand Jury + 3 State AG Suits
sector_alerts.summary: Arizona AG June 1 sued 8 major insurers + MultiPlan for algorithmic price-fixing cartel suppressing out-of-network payments. Lifepoint hospital June 2 federal complaint. DOJ Antitrust Division grand jury subpoena to MultiPlan confirmed May 18. Entities affected: UnitedHealth, Cigna, Aetna, Humana, Elevance, Molina, Centene, HCSC.

sector_alerts.title: Haiti Record Displacement — 1.5 Million IDPs, Hurricane Season Begins
sector_alerts.summary: June 5 IOM: 1.5 million displaced — record high (majority women and girls). Port-au-Prince above 300,000 IDPs for first time. Hurricane season began June 1. 110,000 Haitians forcibly returned from abroad in 2026. Communities previously absorbing displaced families now themselves becoming displacement hotspots.

sector_alerts.title: Sudan Genocide — UAE Arms Supply Documented, Aid Only 40% Funded
sector_alerts.summary: June 8: International investigation documents UAE supplying weapons/mercenaries to RSF fueling Darfur genocide. El Fasher: UN confirmed 6,000+ RSF killings with genocide hallmarks. 28M acutely food insecure. International aid only 40% funded. Lean season June-August expected to worsen. World's largest humanitarian catastrophe entering year 4.

sector_alerts.title: EU AI Act August 2 Enforcement + AI Governance Fragmentation
sector_alerts.summary: EU AI Act high-risk + GPAI enforcement begins August 2, 2026. Scientific Panel (60 experts) appointed June 1. Code of Practice on AI-generated content labeling scheduled June 2026. OpenAI diverges from White House on safety oversight structure (CAISI civilian vs NSA). Anthropic RSP v3 dropped binding pause. AI governance fragmentation across US executive/legislative/EU creates compliance uncertainty.

July 31 deadline for water system Phase 2 claims. Minnesota MPCA new suit over Cottage Grove contamination.

entity_reviews.name: Oracle Corporation
entity_reviews.summary: June 15 WARN Act severance-release deadline for ~30,000 laid-off workers. Oracle absorbed 60-day WARN pay into severance, leaving newer workers with zero net payout. Workers face sign-or-forfeit ultimatum. Retroactive "remote" reclassification to avoid WARN obligations disputed. Active state investigations in WA and MO. Federal WARN Act entitles qualifying workers to 60 days back pay.
